images.title: Edge of Ascent
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.100-400mm F5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: As the Egret departs, it feels like it is falling. Although it does briefly descend, this is all done in order for it to take to the sky.

images.title: Black Vulture
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.100-400mm F5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: I've always been fascinated with Black Vultures. While many species shy away from human presence, the vulture is remarkably bold. It doesn't skirt the edges of our world, but fully ocupies it. Their ancient asthetic, and complex social bonds are a reminder nature doesn't always have to be delicate to be magnificent.

images.title: Dinner, Alone
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: Seeing Ospreys always excites. They are probably my favorite raptor, even if common around the world. This one had found its dinner and was taking it somewhere more private to eat.

images.title: Lizard
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: Seen on the salt flats, the texture of the lizard’s skin begged for a photo.

images.title: PA170012
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, OLYMPUS M.75mm F1.8

images.title: P8200232 Enhanced NR
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.40-150mm F2.8 + MC-20

images.title: Line of Flight
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: As flamingos flew all around us, this one happened to line up perfectly with the layers in the mountains behind.

images.title: Friend
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: As the kayakers moved through the bay, this seal peaked up to check on them.

images.title: Out of the Surf
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: The Oystercatchers were out this day all around us, with this particular one playing by the crashing waves.

images.title: Herb
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5
images.field_notes: Herb. North Atlantic Right Whale #1250. A 40 year veteran of the bay of Fundy, seeing one of these rare creatures in the wild will alter you perception for the rest of your life.

images.title: P2145507
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3

images.title: The Cantilever's Edge
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-3, OLYMPUS M.75mm F1.8

images.title: IMG_0221
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC + MC-14

images.title: The Adjacent Edge
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-3, OLYMPUS M.75mm F1.8

images.title: Nesting
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: The dedication of these parents to raise their young is always a sight to behold. Although she is staying with the nest, dad is always nearby, ready to bring dinner.

images.title: P9250471
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, OLYMPUS M.12-40mm F2.8

images.title: IMG_0381
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5

images.title: IMG_0397
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5

images.title: Mountain Bluebird
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: The electric blue plumage of the Western Bluebird is always striking to see, especially in contrast to the neutrals of the dunes.

images.title: Baltimore Oriole
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: The bright colors of the Baltimore Oriole is always such a delight. The perfect contrast from the orange of the feathers to the blue sky is perfect.

images.title: Born pt. 1
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5
images.field_notes: On this particularly cold day the birds came out after a long night of snow. This White-throated Sparrow shows how it stays warm by poofing up.

images.title: Swallow Swallowing a Swallow
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching the swallows play by the pond, one was spotted still on the branches in the middle. After focusing in on it, the mother came by to feed her young in the right place, right time scenario.

images.title: Hidden Prince
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: A dinosaur of a bird. Listening to thousands of them at the same time is an incredible noise that stays with you.

images.title: IMG_0617
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-3, OLYMPUS M.75mm F1.8

images.title: Window
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.location: The Parklands of Floyds Fork, KY
images.field_notes: Taken by sheer luck, this Belted Kingfisher passed through the hole in the trees for a brief moment.

images.title: The Greatest Egret
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: A beautiful capture of the aigrettes, the fine ornamental feathers, with the sun highlighting them perfect.

images.title: Cormorant Lovers
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: Looking out to the Pacific, this rock was so far away it could barely be discerned what was on it. Imagine my delight after getting home to see these two cuddled up on top.

images.title: P8300636
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-3, OLYMPUS M.75mm F1.8

images.title: Screech Owl
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC + MC-14
images.location: The Parklands of Floyds Fork, KY
images.field_notes: This particular fellow stands guard over the bridge. Everyday hundreds of people pass by, none the wiser to his presence.

images.title: Shared Waters
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching Ospreys hunt is always incredible. Seeing them show up their human counterparts right in front of you is a bonus.

images.title: P9255385 Enhanced NR
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.17mm F1.8

images.title: SEO
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: Watching owls is always a treat, this one was special as it came down to a post nearby, almost as if it was posing.

images.title: Ibis
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 + MC-14
images.field_notes: When a vagrant comes to town, you visit. This one approached within inches after lying in the mud waiting for the right shot.

images.title: P9260611 Enhanced
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, OLYMPUS M.12-40mm F2.8

images.title: Chilean Coast
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.17mm F1.8
images.field_notes: A cliff and the ocean always seem to make sense. Looking down on the beach far below, and seeing the waves roll in, I can practically taste the salty air.

images.title: Garden Bridge
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-3, OLYMPUS M.75mm F1.8
images.field_notes: While in the Japanese Garden this bridge seemed to beckon for its photo to be taken.

images.title: Dissolving Coast
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5
images.field_notes: On the cliffs above, looking down to where the water broke against the rocky shore. The slow shutter speed lets the water soften over the sharp rocks, yet can’t stop the erosion over time.

images.title: Valle de la Luna
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.17mm F1.8
images.field_notes: Valley of the Moon. The name speaks for itself. We were ushered out shortly after this was taken as thunderstorms quickly moved into the area.

images.title: End of the Cape
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5
images.field_notes: The last bit of Cape Cod. The stillness can be felt through this photo.

images.title: Monolith and Man
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5
images.field_notes: The dark, the light, the sand, the wind, the solo hiker.

images.title: Layers of the Atacama
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: Taken on the salt flats, looking toward Bolivia, the flamingos in the foreground add to the scale.

images.title: Washington Light Beams
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, OLYMPUS M.17mm F1.8
images.field_notes: Capturing not only these peaceful beams of light, but also the memory of a morning walk on the coast.

images.title: To Medano
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-3, OM 17mm F1.8 II
images.field_notes: Taken on the dunes, looking out towards a distant mountain lake. You can’t see it, as it’s behind the mountain, but the wish for an alpine dip is still there.

images.title: Curtain Falls
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, OLYMPUS M.17mm F1.8
images.field_notes: A bit of a detour, but one I’m glad to have taken. Earlier this trip I came across a family of three brothers and their father who have taken an annual trip to the BWCA for the last few decades. They expressed regret for not going further to see Curtain Falls. This regret prompted me to change course, and this photograph is for them.

images.title: Steady Beacon
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.17mm F1.8
images.field_notes: A stormy day at the most photographed lighthouse in the world. The winds were blowing strong, and the ocean was crashing, yet the lighthouse still shines.
